column6: Supporting Resilient Livelihoods, Food Security and Climate Adaptation in Yemen (ERRY III) Joint Programme.
column9:
Increased capacities of vulnerable individuals to engage in gender-sensitive and culturally appropriate technical and vocational skills development opportunities through quality apprenticeship program.
?	1,539 apprentices enrolled in on-job training in collaboration with private sector (44% women).
?	308 young men and women trained in solar system installation, repair and maintenance.
?	128 technicians trained in advanced solar pumping systems.
?	6 new vocational curricula developed and 5 updated.

column9: Through a partnership between ILO and Al-Quds University, a youth-focused cooperative business plan competition was launched. It supported youth-led cooperatives in tech, renewable energy, healthcare, and digital media. Winners received training, mentorship, and funding (USD 15,000–25,000). ILO tools Think.COOP and Start.COOP were integrated into university incubation programs.

column7: BGD101 Policies & programmes to promote full, productive & decent employment operationalised.
column9:
ILO support the to DYD:
> Provide technical support and coordinate with the relevant skills training providers to help them attain the required capacity to become RTOs. 
> Lead the process of facilitating the accreditation of RTOs, organise and conduct Training of Trainers in CBT&A NC-IV, and developing learning materials by ensuring the participation of relevant parties. 
> Provide technical support to select NEET youth, women, and People with Disabilities in cooperation with the selected RTOs.
> Deliver skills training at the DYD training centre as well as undertaking monitoring of skill development training programmes at the RTOs; 
> Provide post training Employment Support Service support for industry attachment and job placement of trained graduates.
